hero.title: Datenpunk
hero.tagline: data-driven stories between curiosity and chaos

home.intro: I build data-driven stories at the intersection of analysis, curiosity, and clear communication. This portfolio documents projects where data is not only analysed but made narratively intelligible.
home.prefaceBody: Use this block for a second, longer paragraph — background, how you work, or what readers should know before they dive into the projects. It sits below the opening fold and above the numbered sections.
home.featuredIntro: A glimpse into work where questions, data, and presentation come together.
home.aboutTeaser: My background is in scientific data analysis; I work with Python, R, and SQL. I am interested in how complex datasets become stories that are understandable, visual, and journalistically relevant.

projectsPage.title: Projects
projectsPage.intro: Here I collect data journalism and analytical work — from first experiments to more developed narratives. Each project describes a question, how data was handled, and what follows from it.

projects.title: How absurd are everyday sounds?
projects.teaser: An initial experiment on whether everyday sounds can be distinguished and visualised in a data-driven way.

projects.title: Next project
projects.teaser: Another data journalism piece is in preparation — topic, data basis, and visualisation will follow.

project1.title: How absurd are everyday sounds?
project1.standfirst: An initial experiment on whether everyday sounds can be distinguished and visualised in a data-driven way.
project1.imageAlt: Figure illustrating the everyday sounds project
project1.imageCaption: Visualisation or figure from the project.

question.heading: Question
question.body: Can sounds from daily life — traffic, kitchen, office — be captured and compared so that patterns emerge without losing all sense of lived texture?

data.heading: Data
data.body: Short, anonymised audio clips and derived features (e.g. spectral properties), structured as a table for analysis and later visualisation.

method.heading: Method
method.body: Preparation in Python, exploratory statistics, and a clear decision path: which metrics aid readability, which mainly add noise?

visualisation.heading: Visualisation
visualisation.body: A calm, typography-led presentation of differences between categories — no gimmicks, emphasis on traceability.

conclusion.heading: Conclusion
conclusion.body: A prototype: the question remains open for deeper measurement, but the workflow from raw signal to a narratively usable graphic is documented and extensible.

aboutPage.title: About
aboutPage.intro: I work at the intersection of data, analysis, and communication — aiming to make relationships visible and debatable.
aboutPage.background: Scientific data analysis is the starting point: careful modelling, transparent assumptions, and a willingness to treat uncertainty explicitly. Data journalism adds narrative focus and an audience perspective.
aboutPage.skills: Python, R, and SQL; statistical foundations; research and documentation; visualisation for print and web; structured project planning.
aboutPage.motivation: I am driven by the question of which stories emerge from data when neither sensationalism nor technical isolation is chosen — but clarity, context, and thoughtful design.
aboutPage.contact: For questions or collaboration, please use the channels provided in applications. A public GitHub profile can be added here once it is relevant to the work.

impressumPage.title: Legal notice
impressumPage.lead: This website is a personal portfolio: it presents data-driven articles and projects.
impressumPage.contentBody: This site publishes independent data journalism and analytical work. Nothing here is professional advice in statistics, medicine, law, or any other field. Where available, source code, data snapshots, and working notes are linked via GitHub Projects; GitHub’s own terms apply there.
impressumPage.liabilityContentBody: Portfolio content is prepared with care. No warranty is given for accuracy, completeness, or currency. Liability claims arising from use of the material are excluded to the extent permitted by law.
impressumPage.liabilityLinksBody: The site links to GitHub (projects, repositories). Third-party sites are operated by their respective owners only. No unlawful content was apparent when links were added. Links will be adjusted or removed if violations become known.
impressumPage.copyrightBody: Text, graphics, layout, and data visualisations on this site are protected by copyright unless stated otherwise (for example third-party material in GitHub repositories). Use beyond statutory limits requires the rights holder’s consent.
impressumPage.hostingBody: Pages are served through “GitHub Pages” (GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P. Kelly Jr. Street, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA). Fonts are loaded via Google Fonts (Google Ireland Limited / Google LLC). Technical data may be sent to those providers when you visit. Add a separate privacy policy if you process personal data beyond basic self-presentation.
